content.text: Now let me check for `addTextAnalysis` specifically, since the grep pattern might have missed it.
I now have everything needed to produce the full report. Let me verify the file locations for the service definitions one more time to confirm exact line numbers.

I have all the information needed. Here is the full structured report.

---

## Definition

**File:** `C:\Users\mbrow\git\gpsl-cp-system\discovery-services\apps\discovery-core\src\document\document.controller.ts`
**Lines:** 176–216
**Class:** `DocumentController`

**Signature:**
```typescript
async addDocument(
    @Param('sourceId') sourceId: string,
    @Query('docName', new DefaultValuePipe('tbd')) docName: string,
    @Query('docType', new DefaultValuePipe('pdf')) docType: string,
    @UploadedFile() file: Express.Multer.File,
    @AuditId() auditId: string,
    @Query('collectionId', new ParseArrayPipe({ items: String, optional: true })) collectionIds?: string[],
): Promise<{ success: boolean; transactionId: string }>
```

**Route:** `POST /documents/:sourceId` (multipart/form-data)

**Decorators:** `@Post`, `@UseInterceptors(FileInterceptor)`, `@UseGuards(TokenService)`, `@Transactional('document-add', true)`, `@ApiBearerAuth`

**Summary:** Entry point for uploading a new document. Validates the uploaded file, generates a `dataId` (UUID) and a final document name, assembles a `DocumentImportConfig`, then delegates the entire processing pipeline to `DocumentService.addDocument` via a fire-and-forget async wrapper. Returns `{ dataId }` immediately to the caller while processing continues in the background.

---

## Call Sites

`addDocument` on `DocumentController` is a NestJS route handler. It is invoked by the framework's HTTP router on matching `POST /documents/:sourceId` requests — there are no explicit programmatic call sites in the codebase.

`DocumentService.addDocument` (the service-layer function called internally) has one direct call site:

| File | Line | Enclosing Scope |
|------|------|-----------------|
| `apps/discovery-core/src/document/document.controller.ts` | 211 | `DocumentController.addDocument` (inside `executeAsync` callback) |

`ApiService.addDocument` (the enrichment-layer function called by `DocumentService`) has one direct call site:

| File | Line | Enclosing Scope |
|------|------|-----------------|
| `apps/discovery-core/src/document/document.service.ts` | 145 | `DocumentService.addDocument` |

---

## Internal Calls

These are the functions called inside `DocumentController.addDocument` and, one level deeper, inside `DocumentService.addDocument`.

### `DocumentController.addDocument` (controller layer)

| Callee | Location | Notes |
|--------|----------|-------|
| `this.logger.log` / `this.logger.error` | NestJS built-in `Logger` | Structured log messages with `[TAG]` prefixes |
| `randomUUID` | Node.js `crypto` (line 31 import) | Generates `dataId` and fallback `docName` |
| `this.asyncHelper.executeAsync` | `libs/auditing/src/transaction.utils.ts` line 58 | Fire-and-forget wrapper; marks audit transaction COMPLETED or FAILED in the background |
| `this.documentService.addDocument` | `apps/discovery-core/src/document/document.service.ts` line 51 | Full document processing pipeline (called inside `executeAsync` callback) |

### `DocumentService.addDocument` (service layer — one level deeper)

| Callee | Location | Notes |
|--------|----------|-------|
| `this.documentRepository.createDocument` | `libs/discovery-neo/src/document/document.repository.ts` line 81 | Writes Document node to Neo4j; returns `sourceId` |
| `this.markdownService.parse` | `apps/discovery-core/src/markdown/markdown.service.ts` line 17 | Parses Buffer to `ExtractedDocument` (markdown path) |
| `this.pdfService.import` | `apps/discovery-core/src/pdf/pdf.service.ts` line 34 | Parses Buffer to `ExtractedDocument` (pdf/default path) |
| `this.analysisService.addTextAnalysis` | `apps/discovery-core/src/analysis/analysis.service.ts` line 22 | Mutates `ExtractedDocument` in-place with analysis fields (currently a stub) |
| `this.textService.createTextNodes` | `apps/discovery-core/src/text/text.service.ts` line 27 | Persists paragraph text nodes linked to the document |
| `this.collectionService.associateDocument` | `apps/discovery-core/src/collection/collection.service.ts` line 214 | Links document to a collection in Neo4j (called per collection, **not awaited**) |
| `this.terminologyService.getTerminologyFromCollection` | `apps/discovery-core/src/terminologies/terminology.service.ts` line 211 | Resolves terminology ID for a collection |
| `this.enrichment.addDocument` | `apps/discovery-core/src/enrichment/api.service.ts` line 23 | Triggers enrichment pipeline (embedding, glossary, date extraction) via HTTP |

---

## Data Flow

### Inputs
- `file.buffer` — raw uploaded file bytes passed directly into the PDF or Markdown parser
- `sourceId` — external identifier for the document source, propagated throughout every downstream call
- `collectionIds` — optional array of collection IDs used to associate the document after creation
- `auditId` — injected by `@AuditId()` / `AuditingInterceptor`, used to track the transaction in the auditing database

### External I/O

| Type | Where | Detail |
|------|-------|--------|
| **Neo4j write** | `DocumentRepository.createDocument` | Creates `Document` node (`DOCUMENT_QUERIES.CREATE_DOCUMENT`) |
| **Neo4j write** | `TextService.createTextNodes` | Creates `Text` nodes linked to the document |
| **Neo4j write/read** | `CollectionService.associateDocument` | Creates document-collection relationship |
| **Neo4j read** | `TerminologyService.getTerminologyFromCollection` | Reads terminology ID for a collection |
| **HTTP POST** | `ApiService.triggerEnrichments` → `this.enrichmentServer.post(ENDPOINTS.TRIGGER, payload)` | Calls the enrichment service's `/enrichments/trigger` endpoint with embedding + glossary + date extraction types |
| **HTTP GET** (async polling) | `ApiService.onTransactionComplete` | Polls `ENDPOINTS.TRANSACTION_CHECK/:id` up to 5 times with exponential backoff — fire-and-forget, not awaited by the controller |

### Shared State / Globals
- `DocumentController.logger` — class-level `Logger` instance (read-only for output)
- `DocumentService.logger` — class-level `Logger` instance
- `ENRICHMENT_TRIGGERS.DOCUMENT_ADD` — module-level constant in `api.service.ts` defining which enrichment types are triggered (`[EMBEDDING, GLOSSARY_ADD, DATE_EXTRACTION]`)

---

## Notes

1. **Fire-and-forget at two levels.** `executeAsync` uses `setImmediate` to run the entire document pipeline in the background, so the HTTP response returns before any Neo4j writes or enrichment calls complete. If anything in the pipeline throws, the audit record is marked FAILED but the HTTP client has already received `{ success: true, transactionId }`. Callers must poll the audit transaction to know the true outcome.

2. **`collectionService.associateDocument` is not awaited.** At `document.service.ts` line 132, the call is fire-and-forget within the pipeline — there is no `await`. If it fails, the error is silently lost and the document will not be linked to the collection, but processing continues.

3. **`analyisService.addTextAnalysis` is a stub.** The implementation body (line 22–24 in `analysis.service.ts`) only logs a verbose message and returns nothing. It mutates nothing on the document. This appears to be placeholder code.

4. **`docName` sentinel value.** The string `'tbd'` is used as a sentinel (line 197 in the controller) to auto-generate a UUID-suffixed name. This relies on the query default matching exactly — if a caller intentionally passes `docName=tbd`, it will be treated as a missing name.

5. **No duplicate-document guard at the controller level.** `DocumentRepository.createDocument` does check for an existing document by `sourceId` and returns it silently if found (repository lines 86–99). However, the full pipeline (parsing, text node creation, collection association, enrichment trigger) still executes against the returned `sourceId`. Repeated POSTs with the same `sourceId` will re-process and re-enrich an already-existing document.

6. **Agents used:** No sub-agents were used in producing this report. All analysis was performed directly via Read and Grep tool calls.
